summary: Verified quest image/PDF rendering for TEACHER responses/analysis view (PASS). CHILD side quest detail modal for completed quest is BLOCKED — completed quests are not clickable in QuestsPage, so images/PDF cannot be shown in review mode.

ui_bugs.issue: Completed quests are disabled and not clickable (line 524: onClick={() => !isDisabled && openQuest(quest)}). Since the review request states children must view completed quest in review mode with images/PDF, the modal never opens for a completed quest — image/pdf render code (lines ~668-694) is unreachable for this scenario.

teacher_verification.responses_quest_attachment: PASS - image /api/uploads/quests/587303d3aeb3.png (naturalWidth=512) + 'View attached PDF' link (9fbcb5516779.pdf) rendered inside data-testid='responses-quest-attachment'
teacher_verification.question_analytics_q1_image: PASS - Q1 thumbnail (34aa799583b8.png) rendered in Question Analytics
teacher_verification.student_answers_view: PASS - Q1 image rendered above answers, points display '10/10 pts' (naturalWidth=912), no '/undefined' anywhere
teacher_verification.backend_endpoint: GET /api/teacher/quests/quest_media_test_001/responses returns quest.image_url, quest.pdf_url, question_analytics[].image_url and responses[].question_details[].image_url + max_points + points_earned correctly. All 3 asset URLs return HTTP 200 with correct content-type.

child_verification.quest_list: PASS - Media Test Quest visible in /quests marked COMPLETED
child_verification.quest_detail_modal: FAIL - Modal cannot be opened for completed quest (card disabled). Therefore selectedQuest.image_url / pdf_url / per-question image rendering could not be visually verified from a child user perspective.

seed_data_issue.issue: Seed for classroom 'demo_classroom_1' stored teacher_id='test_teacher_1' (username) but the actual teacher user_id is 't_test_1'. This caused GET /api/teacher/classrooms to return [] and 'Demo Class' did not appear in the Teacher Dashboard. I patched the DB record (updated teacher_id to 't_test_1') to enable UI testing, but the seed script needs to be corrected so future re-seeds don't reintroduce this bug.

action_items: CRITICAL: Update QuestsPage.jsx so completed quests can still be opened in read-only/review mode (allow openQuest for isCompleted, then modal shows images/PDF and hides submit button — has_earned check at line 733 already exists).
Fix seed script to set classrooms.teacher_id to the teacher's user_id ('t_test_1') instead of username ('test_teacher_1'). I patched the running DB, but seed must be corrected for durability.
After the QuestsPage fix, retest child flow to confirm images/PDF render in review-mode modal.
critical_code_review_comments: QuestsPage.jsx line 519-524: isDisabled includes isCompleted, blocking modal open. If the product wants review mode, remove isCompleted from isDisabled or add a separate 'openInReviewMode' path.
Data model mismatch in seed: username vs user_id used inconsistently (classroom teacher_id used username while quest creator_id used user_id) — leads to teacher not seeing his own classroom.
seed_data_creation: Only DB patch: db.classrooms.updateOne({classroom_id:'demo_classroom_1'}, {$set:{teacher_id:'t_test_1'}}) — no new data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Teacher-side rendering is fully working (backend + UI). Child-side quest modal opening for completed quests is blocked by QuestsPage.jsx line 524; needs product/dev decision to allow review-mode. Also seed script has classroom.teacher_id=username bug — I hot-patched DB; if DB is reseeded this must be re-fixed. All backend asset URLs return HTTP 200.
rca_of_the_issue: Frontend QuestsPage.jsx computes isDisabled = isCompleted || isPendingApproval || isExpired (line 519) and the card onClick short-circuits (`!isDisabled && openQuest(quest)`). Since the seed marks Cara G3's response as completed, the card is un-clickable, so the enhanced modal (which does render image_url/pdf_url/per-question image_url) is never mounted. Backend correctly returns image_url and pdf_url in the child's quest detail data — bug is purely on the frontend gating.
